text.title: Workflow Design Phase Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every workflow-design-phase application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: The design phase MUST NOT begin until spec.md frontmatter shows `status: Accepted`. Designing against a Draft spec wastes tokens on decisions that will be re-litigated. The Accepted gate is the human checkpoint protecting the design budget.
rule.rationale: Drafts churn; Accepted specs don't. Starting design on a Draft is the single largest source of wasted SDD effort.

rule.statement: Every AD-X (Architectural Decision) MUST include: (1) the chosen option, (2) at least one rejected alternative with explicit rejection reason, (3) the FR-X / NFR-X requirement it satisfies, (4) the trade-off it accepts. AD records with no rejected alternative are forbidden — they hide the actual decision.
rule.rationale: The value of an ADR is the rejected alternative + rationale. Without those, the record is a status update, not a decision.

rule.statement: The design MUST cite at least 3 existing-codebase patterns it follows (or breaks with rationale). Designs that ignore the existing codebase produce drift; the survey step is non-skippable. Grep/Glob output alone is insufficient — at least one Read of an existing implementation is required per cited pattern.

rationale.source: SDD pattern-drift audit, Conway's Law research

rule.rationale: Greenfield designs in a brownfield codebase create maintenance debt within one sprint. Survey forces alignment.

rule.statement: Every FR-X from spec.md MUST be addressed by at least one AD-X in design.md. Coverage gaps (FRs with no AD trace) MUST be flagged before promoting design to Accepted. Silent drops are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Untraced FRs become forgotten requirements that surface as user-reported regressions post-ship. Explicit coverage map is the only way to catch them pre-execution.

rule.statement: The file table MUST list every file the design will touch with action ∈ {CREATE, MODIFY, DELETE} and a one-sentence scope. Vague entries ("various files", placeholder strings) are forbidden. The file table is the input contract for `writing-implementation-plans`.
rule.rationale: Implementation plans built from vague file tables produce vague tasks; vague tasks produce broken merges. Concrete file table = predictable execution.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
